vibe_and_space: Refined modern Cantonese — dark wood, dim lighting, jewel-box dining rooms. Service polished but warm. Quieter than most Cantonese banquet halls.
one_paragraph_recommendation: Two Michelin stars (2026) and one of the most consistently recommended Cantonese fine-dining options in Shanghai. The honey char siu and crispy suckling pig are the headline dishes; the dim sum service at lunch is a separate experience worth booking. Recommend for a business dinner where you want unmistakable quality without the formality of a tasting menu — also a strong date-night pick in this price tier. Multiple locations exist; the Huangpu / Runan Jie outpost is the flagship.
